rulings.Case Title: 3H RENOVATION SERVICES, LLC ET AL VS. BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Osc Re: Why A Hearing Regarding Contempt Should Not Be Set For Plaintiff Counsel Nedda Haeri'S (1) Failure To Participate In The "Meet And Confer" Per Ccp 430.41; (2) Providing Inconsistent Information Under The Penalty Of Perjury To The Court As To Counsel'S Business Address Location; (3) An Attempt To Engage In Ex-Parte Communication By Partially Contesting The Tentative Ruling Re: Discrepancy Described Above And In The Tentative Ruling Over The Phone Twice (Phone Call On December 28, 2018 And A Message On December 31, 2018);
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday February 8, 2019 line 2. Osc Re: Why A Hearing Regarding Contempt Should Not Be Set For Plaintiff Counsel Nedda Haeri'S (1) Failure To Participate In The "Meet And Confer" Per Ccp 430.41; (2) Providing Inconsistent Information Under The Penalty Of Perjury To The Court As To Counsel'S Business Address Location; (3) An Attempt To Engage In Ex-Parte Communication By Partially Contesting The Tentative Ruling Re: Discrepancy Described Above And In The Tentative Ruling Over The Phone Twice (Phone Call On December 28, 2018 And A Message On December 31, 2018) Hearing required to address the following issues: 1. "If an amended complaint, cross-complaint, or answer is filed, the responding party shall meet and confer again with the party who filed the amended pleading before filing a demurrer to the amended pleading." The operative complaint in this matter was filed on June 5, 2018; Defendant attempted to meet and confer on August 14, 2018 regarding the operative complaint; no amended complaint was filed since; a new meet and confer is required when an amended complaint is filed. Plaintiff 's counsel to address why failed to respond to August 14, 2018 meet and confer letter at any time between August 14, 2018 and November 7, 2018; 2. Per Mr. Labarre's December 28, 2018 declaration Plaintiff's counsel was notified about issues with document arrival on June 5, 2018 and September 10, 2018. In direct contradiction to this declaration Ms. Haeri states that she "only learned of this issue when [it was] mentioned in ¿ subsequently filed demurrer." Counsel to address these specific instances. If June and September communications were in writing, Mr. Labarre may present copies to the Court by 9:00 a.m. on February 8, 2018. 3. Plaintiff counsel states that "all documents from this office were electronically filed and/or served." If this statement was meant to assert that filing was electronic, but service may or may not have been electronic "and/or", this statement is non-responsive to the Court's inquiry. If this statement is indented to mean that all documents were in fact e-served, such claim is not supported by the files on record that the Court was able to locate. The Court was able to locate a number of proofs of service on file attesting to service by mail (e-service box remained unchecked) from the inception of this case to December 31, 2018 (e.g. Opposition to Motion to Vacate; Opposition to Demurrer; December 31, 2018 Declaration). If Plaintiff's counsel is in possession of timely filed file-endorsed proofs of service for e-service for all papers in this matter, counsel shall provide courtesy copies to Department 501 no later than 9:00 a.m. on February 8, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: 3H RENOVATION SERVICES, LLC ET AL VS. BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Osc Re: Why A Hearing Regarding Contempt Should Not Be Set
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday February 8, 2019 line 2. Osc Re: Why A Hearing Regarding Contempt Should Not Be Set 4. As of December 28, 2019 the only Department where Demurrer was calendared was Department 501. Review of the Register of Actions in this action indicates that while matters have been transferred to department 501, they have also been continued to a later date, e.g. Demurrer to Complaint and Motion to Vacate. Not timely arranging for appearance in the department where the motion is calendared is negligence, but is not subject to contempt. 5. Counsel's explanation regarding attempted "ex-parte communication" appears to show counsel's misunderstanding of what constitutes an ex-parte communication. Any communication with the Court regarding the matters presently under the Court's review may be considered improper ex-parte communication if all parties are not properly notified. The issue of the Proof of Service was subject to the Court's tentative ruling and the basis for denial of the Court Call appearance to counsel who appears to be local (based on a declaration under the penalty of perjury) and provides no other reason for inability to appear in person. Any communication with the clerk of the Court regarding a mistake in the declaration would necessarily require this information to be relayed to the judge in order for the judge to authorize the Court Call appearance based on this new information. This communication would be improper ex-parte even though it would not be made directly to the judge. Since counsel's attempt to resolve the matter via the clerk does not appear to be willful and stems from a misunderstanding of ex-parte rules and the extent of clerk's authority, no contempt hearing shall be set on this matter. 6. The Court notes that no courtesy copies of the Response delivered to Department 501 as of 2:50 p.m. on February 7, 2019. = (501/CFH)
